core_mechanism: a two-component (mixture) urn/occupancy model of a population under repeated random sampling, where each round independently retains a fraction (survival rate) and replaces a fraction (coverage rate), yielding closed-form geometric decay of overlap and cumulative-discovery observables, and where a persistent-core-plus-turnover-shell mixture is invoked when a single homogeneous rate cannot fit two projections of the same process simultaneously
brief_reason: The abstract's method rests on an urn model in which each round samples and replaces a fraction of a population (parameters survival alpha and coverage c), gives closed-form containment and discovery curves, and is generalized to a two-component urn with a persistent core fraction kappa when the two projections disagree.
